skill.name: medical-unit-converter
skill.description: Convert medical laboratory values between units (mg/dL to mmol/L, etc.) with formula transparency and clinical reference ranges. Supports glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, and hemoglobin conversions.
skill.summary: Convert medical lab values between unit systems with accurate formulas and clinical reference ranges.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The 32 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are medical conversion factors (e.g., 0.0555 for glucose, 38.67 for cholesterol), not cryptographic material. The 43 'shell backtick execution' detections are standard markdown code block formatting. The URL on line 7 of SKILL.md is a legitimate source attribution link. The 'system reconnaissance' flag is standard error handling text. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns were found. The skill is a legitimate medical unit converter with a Python script using argparse and hardcoded conversion factors.

content.user_title: Convert Medical Lab Values Between Units
content.value_statement: Medical professionals and researchers often need to convert laboratory values between different unit systems like mg/dL and mmol/L. This skill provides accurate conversions with formula transparency and clinical reference ranges for common analytes.
actual_capabilities: Convert glucose values between mg/dL and mmol/L with fasting reference ranges
Convert cholesterol values between mg/dL and mmol/L with desirable range guidance
Convert creatinine values between mg/dL and umol/L with sex-specific reference ranges
Convert hemoglobin values between g/dL and g/L with clinical reference ranges
Convert triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, and potassium between unit systems
Validate inputs and return structured results with formula transparency
limitations: Only supports the predefined analytes documented in the conversion table
Does not interpret clinical results or diagnose medical conditions
Cannot convert between unit pairs that are not in the conversion dictionary
Does not support custom conversion factors or user-defined unit pairs

use_cases.title: Clinical Documentation Review
use_cases.description: Convert lab values from medical records to standard units for consistent documentation across different healthcare systems.

use_cases.title: Research Data Harmonization
use_cases.description: Normalize lab results from different sources to a single unit system for aggregate analysis in clinical studies.

use_cases.title: Patient Education Materials
use_cases.description: Convert lab results to patient-friendly units when creating educational content or explaining results to patients.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Glucose Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert 180 mg/dL glucose to mmol/L. Include the reference range.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Lab Value Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these lab values: glucose 126 mg/dL, cholesterol 240 mg/dL, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, hemoglobin 14.5 g/dL. Convert all to their SI units and include reference ranges.

prompt_templates.title: Conversion with Bidirectional Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: A lab report shows cholesterol of 5.7 mmol/L. Convert to mg/dL and verify the result by converting it back. Show the formula for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Research Data Normalization
prompt_templates.prompt: I have hemoglobin values in g/dL from a clinical study: 15.2, 13.8, 14.1, 12.9, 16.0. Convert all values to g/L, provide the reference range, and flag any values outside the normal range.

output_examples.output: Glucose: 100 mg/dL to 5.6 mmol/L. Formula: 100 x 0.0555. Reference range: 3.9 to 5.6 mmol/L (fasting glucose). This value falls within the normal fasting range.
Total Cholesterol: 240 mg/dL to 6.2 mmol/L. Formula: 240 x 0.02586. Reference range: less than 5.2 mmol/L (desirable). This value exceeds the desirable range.

output_examples.input: Convert creatinine 1.2 mg/dL to umol/L for a male patient
output_examples.output: Creatinine: 1.2 mg/dL to 106.1 umol/L. Formula: 1.2 x 88.42. Reference range: 62 to 115 umol/L (male). This value is within the normal range.

best_practices: Always specify the analyte name to get the correct clinical reference range alongside the converted value.
Verify important results by converting in both directions to confirm the conversion factor was applied correctly.
Use the Python script directly for batch conversions of large lab result tables in research workflows.
anti_patterns: Using this skill for clinical diagnosis or treatment decisions instead of consulting a qualified healthcare provider.
Attempting to convert non-laboratory measurements or requesting analytes not in the supported conversion table.
Relying on a single converted value without cross-referencing with established clinical guidelines or reference materials.

faq.question: What analytes does this skill support?
faq.answer: Glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, hemoglobin, triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, and potassium.

faq.question: What unit conversions are available for glucose?
faq.answer: Glucose can be converted between mg/dL and mmol/L using a factor of 0.0555 (mg/dL to mmol/L) or 18.018 (mmol/L to mg/dL).

faq.question: Does this skill provide clinical interpretation of lab values?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides reference ranges alongside conversions but does not diagnose conditions or interpret clinical results.

faq.question: Can I add custom conversion factors?
faq.answer: No. Only predefined conversion pairs are supported. The conversion dictionary is hardcoded in the Python script.

faq.question: What happens if I request an unsupported conversion?
faq.answer: The skill will list all supported conversions and stop. It will not fabricate conversion factors that are not in the dictionary.

faq.question: What tools can run this skill?
faq.answer: This skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
